promote_if: Explicit downloadable dataset, database, registry, workbook, table, code/data repository, source-family page, file inventory, package data object, or challenge/venue group with multiple records.
reject_or_route_if: Only replication wording in title/abstract without files, rows, or source-family structure; methods/commentary/news without reusable source object; one replication paper without multi-row artifact.
why: Prevents generic replication-word pollution while preserving reusable source objects.

promote_if: Package is for a replication/follow-up paper that independently evaluates an original result, or contains multiple original-replication pairs/source-family inventory.
reject_or_route_if: Title patterns like Replication Data for, Replication Files for, Replication package for, or Data and code for where associated article is the original empirical paper.
why: Many disciplines use replication package to mean original-paper reproducibility materials.

promote_if: Record contains original DOI/PMID/title/authors plus replication/follow-up DOI/report/target identifier, or fields such as original_effect, replication_effect, original_n, replication_n, target_study, replicated_finding, claim_id, outcome_id.
reject_or_route_if: Only robustness, validation, or reanalysis without a target original result; one-dataset alternative analyses unless Figure 1 explicitly allows robustness-only pairs.
why: Figure 1 requires paired original and larger-N replication/follow-up evidence.

promote_if: Lead provides effect sizes, test statistics, p-values, confidence intervals, sample sizes, site/lab counts, raw data sufficient to compute them, or codebook/schema naming effect and N columns.
reject_or_route_if: Only binary success/failure labels and no effect/sample evidence unless high-value corpus candidate; qualitative comments only.
why: Flags which discovered source objects can actually support extraction.

promote_if: Replication refers to study/result/claim/finding replication or computational reproduction.
reject_or_route_if: Database/storage/data-system replication; DNA/RNA/viral/cell replication; manufacturing or duplicate-copying uses unless scholarly result replication is explicit.

promote_if: Landing page is a named programme, consortium, challenge, registry, database, gateway, package, curated list, or enumerates multiple targets/reports/datasets.
reject_or_route_if: Single article with one original and one replication result routes to individual-paper worklist; review/editorial without structured table routes context.
why: Keeps source-family table focused on reusable discovery objects.

promote_if: OpenReview/ReScience/challenge group has multiple reports, stable group/issue/venue identifier, original-paper links, code, metrics, or comparison tables.
reject_or_route_if: Individual challenge report without reusable rows routes individual-paper; challenge announcement without accepted reports/data routes context.
why: Turns MLRC/ReScience into small source-family objects rather than thousands of paper hits.

promote_if: Derived dataset adds crosswalks, normalized fields, or links back to original projects and source studies.
reject_or_route_if: Derived dataset merely rehosts canonical RPP/EERP/SSRP/Many Labs records with no new fields; mark duplicate and retain alias.
why: Preserves useful aggregators without double-counting effects.

promote_if: Funder/project registry links to completed outputs, datasets, reports, or source-family pages.
reject_or_route_if: Award/grant only with no outputs routes manual investigation; prospective incomplete project routes context.
why: Funder databases are discovery surfaces, not provenance endpoints.

promote_if: Dataverse/Zenodo/Figshare/OSF collection is organized around a replication project, source family, challenge, or special section with multiple related datasets and project-level description.
reject_or_route_if: Generic journal replication-data collection where each dataset is original-paper reproducibility material; individual dataset with no independent follow-up evidence.
why: Avoids treating journal data archives as false Figure 1 corpora.
